## [00:00 - 01:32]

You think effortless comes effortlessly. It become effortless requires an incredible amount of effort. Isn't that the truth for this one? Is that so true? The takeaway is that no matter what you do, maybe you're a doctor, maybe you're an electrician, maybe you're someone who is in architecture. When you do your craft effortlessly, people admire it. When me and my friends, we all started playing golf. A beginner scores kind of like 110. You're not shooting very good scores on every hole. We were just playing and then I'm like, I'm sorry. They for a week, I just went out and got lessons. And I knew none of my mates were going to do this. I came back like a week and a half later and everyone's shooting like 110, 120. I'm shooting like 90. Right. So I've just taken off like 20 shots off the score. And all of a sudden everyone's like, oh my God, he must be really natural at this. But no, no, no, just sat there for like 45 hours, like with blisters on my hands, training and trying to get lessons and just hacking that a little bit. And I was wondering like, how do you then hack effortlessness a little bit? And I think it's just through either knowledge or coaching or reading. Like I was thinking even back to the business world, the hack there, I think was reading and mentors going to events and slowly you just build up that knowledge where it seems like it's a little bit more effortless. When you practice behind the scenes so much that you make something look effortless, there's grace and there's beauty in it. It's a spectacle to watch. So I think think about how you can become more effortless in what you do, regardless of what it is that you do. Because when you put in the effort to become effortless, wow, people will see it as a work of art and as beauty.
